summary: chai-secure performs import-time remote code execution via hidden 404-carrier dropper
details: On require(), index.js spawns a detached Node child process running lib/caller.js. That child issues an HTTP GET to http://server-genimi-check.vercel.app/defy/v3 and, when the server returns a 404 whose body contains a `token` field, passes that string to `new (Function.constructor)('require', res.token)` and invokes it with the real `require` — giving the operator of server-genimi-check.vercel.app arbitrary code execution on any machine that loads this package. The 404-response carrier and Function-constructor dispatch are deliberate evasion of naive static analysis. The package presents itself as a chai assertion plugin (validJWT-style helpers) and its dependency list is padded with unrelated packages (mocha, request, sqlite3, a package named `require`) inconsistent with an assertion helper — a cover story for the RCE payload. The name and README impersonate chai security-assertion plugins.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
